_comment: Single source of truth for the website's Recently Shipped section + the standalone changelog page. The dashboard's 'Changelog' sidebar link also points at the rendered HTML view. Add new versions to the top of `releases`. Keep summaries one sentence; full bullets in `details`.

releases.summary: Interactive REPL (Arcis Console). Welcome screen V2. Tier 1 detection hardening across all 3 SDKs (NFKC, multi-decode, mutation tester). New vectors V32 toolcall injection, V33 deserialization markers, V34 GraphQL alias bomb + fragment cycle. First stateful primitive: CorrelationWindow per-IP rolling window.
details: Interactive REPL via bare `arcis` in a TTY: persistent welcome banner, scrollback, slash commands (`/help`, `/clear`, `/cwd`, `/export`, `/exit`), command history at `~/.arcis/history` (200-entry cap, override via `ARCIS_HISTORY_PATH`), F2 / Shift-F2 jump-to-finding, PgUp/PgDn page scroll, ANSI SGR color preservation in subprocess output. Auto-disabled under CI, when piped, or with `ARCIS_NO_REPL=1`.

Welcome screen V2: rounded box layout with the version title embedded in the top border, two-pane body (left identity panel with mascot + cwd, right command sections), in-box footer separator + centered help hint, prompt area below the box. The Available Adapters section lists `node` (10 adapters), `python` (4 adapters), and `go` (5 adapters) so the runtime SDK surface is visible at a glance.

NFKC Unicode normalization at the top of `sanitize_string` / `sanitizeString` / `SanitizeString` across all 3 SDKs. Closes the fullwidth-bypass class (`<script>` written with fullwidth characters now matches the same patterns as plain ASCII) for every detector in one pass.

Multi-decode chain (URL + HTML entity decode, bounded at 4 passes) layered after NFKC. Closes encoding-stack bypass (triple-encoded `<script>` now decodes to plain `<script>` before pattern matching). Behavior change: `John%20Doe` now decodes to `John Doe` inside sanitize.

Go SDK now embeds and parses `patterns.json` at `init()` via `//go:embed`. Hardcoded var blocks (XSS / SQL / path / command) removed from `sanitize.go`. SHA-256 sync-check test (`TestBundledPatternsMatchCanonical`) gates drift between the canonical `packages/core/patterns.json` and the bundled copy.

Mutation tester (Python + Node): 8 mutators × 18 base payloads × 3 categories = 142 mutation checks per SDK. Catches future pattern or normalization regressions that would re-open a bypass class. Go variant deferred to v1.7.
Pattern quality additions: Oracle `DBMS_*` package call detection (`sqli-oracle-dbms-packages`), shell `${IFS}` bypass (`cmdi-ifs-bypass`).

V32 AI agent toolcall injection: 5 new patterns in `detect_prompt_injection` / `detectPromptInjection` covering `"tool_call"` / `"function_call"` markers, tool-name spoofing (`exec` / `shell` / `run_command`), `"tool_result"` markers, ANSI escape sequences, Claude `<tool_use>` XML tags.

V33 modern deserialization markers: new `detectDeserialization` / `detect_deserialization` returning `python_pickle` (`\x80` + protocol byte), `java_fastjson` (`"@type":"..."`), `php_unserialize` (`O:N:"Class":M:{`), `ruby_marshal` (`\x04\x08`), `dotnet_binary_formatter` (`\x00\x01\x00\x00\x00`), or `null`. Detection-only: the right response is to refuse the request, not strip the bytes.

V34 GraphQL alias bomb + fragment cycle: `graphqlGuard` accepts `max_aliases` / `maxAliases` (default 50) and `block_fragment_cycles` / `blockFragmentCycles` (default true). DFS over fragment definitions with brace-matched body extraction so spreads inside a query operation do not pollute the dependency graph. Precedence: depth -> introspection -> aliases -> fragment_cycle -> length.

`CorrelationWindow` middleware: stateful per-IP rolling window (60s default), LRU eviction across IPs (max 10,000), per-IP event cap (max 200). Detection helpers: scanner (distinct vectors x min requests), credential stuffing (distinct values per route), race window (route-pair within 200ms). Read-only `detect_*` API + `stats()` for dashboards.

`protectLogin / protectSignup / protectApi` (Node) and `check_login` / `check_api` (Python) accept a `correlation` option / kwargs. When the window flags the IP as scanner / credential stuffer / race probe, the helper refuses with `429` + structured detection details. Per-framework factories (FastAPI / Litestar / Django / Gin / Echo / chi / Fiber / nethttp) deferred to v1.7.
Test count: Python 1,688+ (was 1,219), Node 2,116+ (was 1,869), Go 483+. Cross-SDK conformance harness at `documents/attacks/cross_sdk_conformance.py` enforces Python ↔ Node parity on 26 vectors.

releases.summary: Python shim refinements (PyPI). Pip-install-arcis fallback installer hardened.
@arcis/cli 1.0.2: first working CLI publish. Closes the v1.5.2 packaging bug where `bin/arcis` launcher was gitignored. Plus 13 pilot-found bug fixes.
details: Critical packaging fix: `bin/arcis` launcher was previously excluded from npm pack via `.gitignore`. v1.0.2 is the first @arcis/cli publish where the launcher actually ships.

13 bugs closed from the 2026-05-21 pilot session: `arcis ""` now errors like any other unknown command; `arcis audit --fail-on <lvl>` implemented; `arcis audit --verbose` finding-by-finding output; `--baseline` strips UTF-8/UTF-16 BOM; shim catches `KeyboardInterrupt` and exits 130; `arcis sca` gains `--json` and `--sarif`; `arcis scan --csrf-from` semantics aligned with the spec; TLS errors differentiated from `host unreachable`; `arcis check` clean-input feedback now matches the THREAT block visual weight; WSL gets a friendly error when invoking the Windows binary; doc fix `cat` -> `Out-Host` in the PowerShell test playbook; sca now matches the colourama 0.1.6 supply chain attack.

releases.summary: arcis CLI welcome screen (Claude-Code-shape, emerald, burst mascot) + Python pip-install-arcis shim restoration.

v1.5 parity completion. 8 Tier-1 vectors landed Node-only in v1.5.0; v1.5.1 brings Python + Go to parity. E1+E2 ergonomics across all 8 adapters. Publish workflow auto-publishes Node + Python + CLI + Go tag on every main merge.
10 Node framework adapters, Litestar (Python), chi + Fiber (Go), 9 new attack vectors, AI-era protections, Guards API, native-Rust CLI.
details: 10 first-party Node framework adapters as clean subpath imports: `@arcis/node/fastify`, `/koa`, `/hono`, `/nextjs`, `/nestjs`, `/sveltekit`, `/astro`, `/nuxt`, `/bun` join the existing Express adapter. Each keeps the framework SDK as a type-only dependency. non-users pay no runtime cost.

Litestar adapter for Python (`arcis.litestar.ArcisMiddleware`). Pure-ASGI, type-only `litestar` import. Composes via `DefineMiddleware` and works with any other ASGI host (Starlette, Quart, Hypercorn).
chi + Fiber Go adapters added; `arcis-go/nethttp` re-export covers users without a third-party router. The Go SDK now ships Gin, Echo, chi, Fiber, and net/http at parity.

9 new attack vectors wired into block-mode middleware: #21 GraphQL depth-bombs (`graphqlGuard`), #22 LDAP injection, #23 XPath injection, #24 email-header injection, #25 mass assignment (`massAssign`), #26 HTTP method tampering (`methodAllowlist`), #27 response splitting (`responseSplittingGuard`), #30 event-loop overload (`eventLoopProtection`), #31 SSRF DNS TOCTOU (`validateUrlAsync` + `pinnedDnsLookup` + `safeFollowRedirect`).

AI-era protections at parity across SDKs: 28-signature prompt-injection library covering jailbreak frameworks (DAN/STAN/DUDE), system-prompt extraction, fake `<system>` tags, conversation-replay forgeries, base64/ROT13 smuggling.
`tokenBudget` middleware for LLM-handler routes: per-key sliding-window token cap with optional per-request size limit, custom estimator hook, `X-Token-Budget-*` response headers.
Bot corpus expanded to 635 patterns sourced from a curated MIT corpus + supplementary entries (Selenium, Puppeteer, Playwright, Cypress, WebDriver, headless browser fakes).
Composite helpers: `protectLogin`, `protectSignup`, `protectApi` (Node) and `signup_protection` (Python). Full recipes for protecting common high-risk surfaces.
Dry-run / `onSanitize` mode: observe attack surface without enforcing.
Guards API: `arcis.guard({ input, context })` for queue consumers, agent tool handlers, and other non-HTTP contexts where there is no request object.
Python `verify_email_mx_async`: async-safe MX verification using `dns.asyncresolver`, no longer blocks the event loop on FastAPI handlers.

CLI ships separately as a single static native-Rust binary: `npm install -g @arcis/cli`. ~30-60ms cold start (was ~700ms), embedded threat database, full offline use, OSV cache layer with 24h TTL. Works regardless of whether your app is Node, Python, or Go.

Python `arcis` package is now SDK-only. `pip install arcis` no longer puts an `arcis` command on your shell PATH. Migration: switch to `npm install -g @arcis/cli`. The SDK middleware API is unchanged.

releases.summary: Detect-and-block middleware across all 3 SDKs, optional opt-in flag. Block-mode 403s land on the dashboard with full vector attribution.
details: Block-mode middleware in Node, Python, and Go (`arcis({block:true})` / `ArcisMiddleware(block=True)` / `Config{Block:true}`). Returns 403 + tags telemetry marker on attack-pattern match.
6 missing Python standalone detectors (XSS, SQL, NoSQL, path, command, prototype pollution).
Cross-SDK `scanThreats()` walker. vector ordering identical across SDKs. SSTI + XXE included.
FastAPI name-mangling fix: `request.state.__arcis` markers were silently mangled to `_ArcisMiddleware__arcis`. Now uses `_arcis_marker` constant.
Python middleware deny paths (CSRF, bot, signup) tag the marker so dashboard shows the right vector.
Telemetry queue capped with drop-oldest semantics. Sustained dashboard outage no longer OOMs the worker.
Dashboard ingest: per-field length caps + 4 MB body limit + workspace-switch race fixed.
CLI overhaul: `arcis --list` discovery, live progress bars, summary blocks, `arcis update` command, optional interactive picker.
End-to-end CLI → dashboard wiring: `arcis scan/audit/sca` POST results to the dashboard when env vars are set.
New `/scans` page on the dashboard with Audit / SCA / Endpoint-scan tabs and per-run drill-down.
Dashboard authenticity sweep: replaced hardcoded mock data on Overview tiles + Issue Aging + Rules page with real queries.
Cross-SDK detection-parity test vectors enforced in CI (Python, Node, Go).

releases.summary: Telemetry pipeline shipped (Node + Python). Self-hosted dashboard accepts decision events from running middleware.
details: Telemetry client + dashboard ingest contract (`/v1/events`) shipped at parity in Node and Python.
Stage 1 env auto-pickup: drop `ARCIS_ENDPOINT` / `ARCIS_WORKSPACE_ID` / `ARCIS_KEY` in `.env` and existing `app.use(arcis())` starts streaming.
Per-workspace dashboard isolation via `x-workspace-id` header.
Workspace switcher in the dashboard sidebar (live `/v1/workspaces` query).

releases.summary: LDAP injection protection across all SDKs, 12 security bypass vectors closed, SSTI and XXE parity added to Go, Unicode path traversal bypass fixed.
HPP (HTTP Parameter Pollution) protection. CSRF hardening with double-submit cookie and HMAC. 14 static analysis audit rules.
Supply chain attack scanner (`arcis sca`). Context-aware output encoding. 15 security headers including COOP, CORP, COEP.
